data.title: McDonald's is bringing back its popular Snack Wrap menu item this year
data.description:
McDonald's (NYSE:MCD) plans to bring back its popular Snack Wrap menu item sometime in 2025, according to a cryptic social media post. The restaurant chain teased that the launch would be on the 14th, but did not specify which month. 

The Snack Wrap, which has been off the McDonald's (NYSE:MCD) menu for almost a decade, was first introduced in the United States in 2006 and quickly became a fan favorite due to its convenient, handheld format and lighter profile compared to traditional burgers. The original version featured either crispy or grilled chicken, shredded lettuce, cheese, and a choice of ranch or honey mustard sauce, all wrapped in a soft flour tortilla.  Over the last five years, various social media posts asking McDonald's (MCD) to bring back Snack Wrap have gone viral.

The portability and satisfying taste of Snack Wrap helped boost McDonald's (MCD) sales almost immediately. However, despite the popularity of Snack Wrap, the restaurant chain discontinued it in 2016 due mainly to operational inefficiencies. The assembly process was considered too time-consuming for the chain’s fast-paced kitchens, and the wrap ultimately didn’t meet internal sales expectations. 

The revived Snack Wrap is expected to feature some updates, aligning with contemporary tastes while preserving the qualities that made it a fan favorite in the first place

While the exact launch date remains under wraps for competitive reasons, the Chicago-based company has confirmed that the item will be back before the end of the year in what could be a noteworthy development for fast-food rivals such as Burger King (QSR), Taco Bell (YUM), KFC (YUM), Wendy's (WEN), Shake Shack (SHAK), and Jack in the Box (JACK) scrapping for market share.

data.title: SUNation Energy sinks after approving 200-for-1 reverse stock split
data.description:
SUNation Energy (NASDAQ:SUNE) -48.8% in Wednesday's trading after saying its board approved a 200-for-1 reverse stock split effective April 21, just six months after the seller of photovoltaic solar energy systems and battery storage systems executed a 50-for-1 reverse split.

SUNation Energy (NASDAQ:SUNE) said the reverse split is intended to allow it to regain compliance with the minimum bid price required for continued listing on the Nasdaq Capital Market, after shares have plunged 99% YTD.

A week ago, the company said it raised ~$20M in aggregate gross proceeds via a securities purchase agreement with certain institutional investors, helping eliminate $12.6M of secured debt and other long-term contractual obligations.

data.title: Fed's 'modestly restrictive' policy stance apt for taming inflation, Hammack says
data.description:
Federal Reserve Bank of Cleveland President Beth Hammack signaled Wednesday her support for maintaining the current "modestly restrictive" monetary policy stance to bring inflation down to the central bank's goal. 

Like many of her colleagues, Hammack wants to stay the course on the Fed's wait-and-see approach as high tariff uncertainty continues to cloud the economic outlook.

"With inflation elevated, the current modestly restrictive stance for monetary policy is appropriate to continue the downward pressure on inflation so that it returns to our goal in a timely fashion," she said in remarks prepared for delivery at a Columbus Metropolitan Club event in Columbus, Ohio.

However, should the economy stumble and inflation fall, then rates cuts may be appropriate, she said. Conversely, "monetary policy may need to follow a more restrictive trajectory" if the labor market stays healthy and inflation rises persistently.

A more stagflationary dynamic of higher inflation and a cooling labor market, meanwhile, would result in "some challenging tradeoffs" for monetary policy, she said. "In that case, it will be important to ensure inflation expectations remain well anchored while assessing the likely magnitude and persistence of the misses to each side of our dual mandate goals."

"I would rather be slow and move in the right direction than move quickly in the wrong direction," Hammack said. "Fortunately, I think we are in position to assess the incoming data, the risks to the outlook, and the appropriate policy response to achieve our longer-term objectives. If only the path to meet both parts of our mandate was as clear as following the yellow brick road."

data.title: Seeking Alpha’s top Quant picks in Energy sector as Q1 earnings loom
data.description:
The S&P 500's Energy sector (NYSEARCA:XLE), which holds a 4.2% weightage on the S&P 500 Index and includes companies that are into oil production, drilling, refining and transportation, gained nearly 8% during the first quarter, compared to over 4% decline in the broader market.

According to FactSet, the Energy sector is expected to report the largest (year-over-year) earnings decline of all eleven sectors at -13.7%. This is primarily due to lower oil prices, with Q1 2025 averaging $71.38, down 10% from Q1 2024’s $76.91.

At the sub-industry level, three of the five sub-industries in the sector are predicted to report a year-over-year decline in earnings: Oil & Gas Refining & Marketing (-103%), Integrated Oil & Gas (-13%), and Oil & Gas Equipment & Services (-9%), said the report.

On the other hand, two sub-industries are predicted to report year-over-year growth in earnings: Oil & Gas Storage & Transportation (13%) and Oil & Gas Exploration & Production (10%), it added.

For Q1 2025, the estimated Y/Y earnings growth rate for the S&P 500 is above 10%.

According to the latest data from Seeking Alpha's Quant Rating System, stocks making up the energy index have secured an average health score of 3.32. The system awards grades based on quantitative measures, like valuation, earnings growth and recent stock performance. The highest possible score for any individual company is a 5.

Looking at individual companies in the sector, based on their quant ratings, one out of 22 stocks is rated as a Strong Buy and two as a Buy, while 19 stocks are rated Neutral. None of the stocks are considered Sell or lower.

Exxon Mobil (XOM) is leading with the highest Quant rating of 4.76, with profitability and momentum factors boosting its ratings. The company, in January 2025, posted better than expected Q4 adjusted earnings, driven by high-margin projects in the Permian Basin and Guyana.

ConocoPhillips (COP) stands in the second spot with a score of 4.16 and a Buy rating.

Coterra Energy (CTRA), Expand Energy (EXE) and EQT Corporation (EQT) follow with Quant scores of 4.09, 3.46 and 3.41, respectively.

Halliburton (HAL) has the lowest Quant score of 2.87 in the sector. Shares of the U.S. oilfield services firm declined over 21% so far this year.

data.title: Tesla loses traction in California with registrations down for sixth straight quarter
data.description:
Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) registrations in California fell for the sixth consecutive quarter, dropping 15.1% year-over-year, even as overall new car registrations in the state rose 8.3%, according to Experian Automotive data.

“Tesla’s troubles continue to worsen as Californians are giving the cold shoulder to the direct-to-consumer automaker (and controversial owner, Elon Musk),” the California New Car Dealers Association said in response to the results.

With registrations down, Tesla’s market share was down by 11.6% at the end of Q1 to 43.9%, now holding less than half of the California Zero Emission Vehicle (“ZEV”) market for the year. This weighed on overall ZEV sales momentum, with a second quarterly decline in registrations at just 20.8% of the total market share, down from 22% a year ago.

“Dealers sell what customers want to buy. No mandate can force consumers to choose otherwise. Although the manufacturers we represent are increasing EV sales in California, with the substantial decline in Tesla sales, EV market penetration is largely flat. This puts us well short of EV sales mandates that take effect this year,” said Robb Hernandez, CNCDA’s Chairman.

For the full year, overall registrations are expected to drop 2.3% to 1.71M vehicles.

Among total California vehicle registrations, Toyota (TM) held the top spot with a 16.5% market share, Honda (HMC) took second with 10.8%, with Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) in third place at 9.1%.

data.title: Progressive stock gains after number of policies in Q1 climb 18% Y/Y
data.description:
Progressive's (NYSE:PGR) stock gained 2.0% in Wednesday midday trading after its Q1 policies in force exceeded the Wall Street consensus.

Policies in force jumped to 36.3M at March 31, 2025, rose 18% Y/Y, topping the Visible Alpha consensus of 36.0M. Direct auto policies made the biggest jump, rising 25% Y/Y to 14.8M policies.

Q1 GAAP EPS of $4.37 climbed 11% Y/Y from $3.94 in the year-ago quarter. Comprehensive income of $5.90 per share jumped from $3.59 in the year-ago period.

Net premiums written rose 17% to $22.2B from $19.0B.

Net premiums earned increased 20% Y/Y to $19.4B from $16.1B in last year's Q1.

Combined ratio of 86.0% improved from 86.1% in the year-ago period.

Total expenses of $17.2B rose from $14.3B in last year's Q1.

Progressive's Q1 conference call will be held on May 6 at 9:30 AM ET.

data.title: Autoliv outlines 2025 guidance with 10-10.5% operating margin target amid efficiency gains
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Autoliv (ALV) Q1 2025

Management View

  • CEO Mikael Bratt highlighted a solid first quarter driven by Autoliv's adaptability and resilience. He noted that the company outperformed global light vehicle production despite significant headwinds in China. Key drivers included cost reduction programs cutting over 1,500 indirect and 3,700 direct workforce positions year-over-year, and agreements with customers to neutralize tariffs.
  • Bratt reported record earnings per share for the quarter, a high return on capital employed, and a strong balance sheet enabling $50 million in share repurchases and a $0.70 per share dividend payout.
  • CFO Fredrik Westin detailed a 28% increase in adjusted operating income, reaching $255 million, and a 230 basis point improvement in the adjusted operating margin to 9.9%. He also reported that operating cash flow was $77 million despite higher receivables from strong sales.

Outlook

  • Management reaffirmed the full-year 2025 guidance of a 10%-10.5% adjusted operating margin and an operating cash flow forecast of approximately $1.2 billion. The guidance assumes a global light vehicle production decline of around 0.5% and a tax rate of roughly 28%.
  • CEO Bratt described the outlook as challenging due to uncertainties surrounding tariffs, economic growth, and regional variations in production. He emphasized Autoliv's focus on efficiency and profitability improvements to sustain strong shareholder returns.
  • Bratt stated the expectation for a significant improvement in Chinese sales in the second half of 2025, driven by upcoming product launches.

Financial Results

  • Q1 2025 net sales reached $2.6 billion, a 1% year-over-year decrease due to currency effects and regional production challenges. Organic sales grew by 2%.
  • Gross margin increased to 18.6%, up 160 basis points year-over-year, primarily due to enhanced labor productivity and headcount reductions.
  • Adjusted return on equity was reported at 29%, supported by share buybacks and strong net profit.
  • Regional sales performance showed notable growth in China for domestic OEMs, but overall underperformance in the Chinese market due to a shift in the light vehicle production mix.

Q&A

  • Colin Langan, Wells Fargo, inquired about the impact of USMCA non-compliance on sales. CEO Bratt stated that the situation remains fluid, with a small part of production impacted by non-compliance. He reassured that cost burdens from tariffs are passed on to customers.
  • Emmanuel Rosner, Wolfe, asked about the decision to maintain guidance amid uncertainty. Bratt explained that strong first-quarter performance and visibility into the second quarter supported the company’s confidence in the current guidance.
  • Edison Yu, Deutsche Bank, questioned the quick pass-through of tariff-related costs. Bratt affirmed that negotiations with customers would ensure timely cost recovery.
  • Chris McNally, Evercore, sought clarity on regional performance. Bratt highlighted Asia as the strongest growth driver, with North America and Europe facing challenges due to economic conditions.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed concerns over tariff impacts and the potential regional production declines. Their tone was pressing and skeptical regarding the company's ability to navigate these challenges without margin erosion.
  • Management maintained a confident tone in addressing questions, emphasizing adaptability and efficiency measures. CEO Bratt repeatedly stressed Autoliv’s commitment to passing on tariff-related costs to customers and mitigating risks.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, analysts’ tone appeared more focused on the risks of tariffs, while management’s confidence remained steady.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance language shifted from emphasizing gradual margin improvements throughout the year to highlighting significant uncertainty in Q1 2025.
  • Focus on workforce reductions and efficiency gains continued, with an incremental $50 million cost-saving target reaffirmed.
  • Analysts' questions in this quarter showed heightened scrutiny on geopolitical and tariff-related impacts compared to the previous quarter, where inflation and cost recoveries were central themes.
  • Management’s confidence in navigating tariff challenges was more pronounced this quarter.

Risks and Concerns

  • Tariff impositions remain a key uncertainty. Management outlined strategic measures, including customer negotiations, localized sourcing, and leveraging their U.S. footprint to mitigate impacts.
  • Regional production mix shifts, particularly in China, continue to pose a challenge to sales performance.
  • Labor cost increases in Europe and the Americas were flagged as ongoing pressures.

Final Takeaway

Autoliv presented a strong Q1 2025 performance with record EPS and solid operational improvements. Despite challenges from tariffs and regional production shifts, management reaffirmed its guidance for 2025, supported by efficiency gains and a robust balance sheet. Confidence in navigating complex market conditions while maintaining shareholder returns was a key theme throughout the call.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Citizens Financial targets 3.05% to 3.10% NIM by year-end 2025 with strategic initiatives
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Citizens Financial Group (CFG) Q1 2025

Management View

  • CEO Bruce Van Saun highlighted a 3 basis point expansion in net interest margin (NIM) to 2.90% and core loan growth of 1% for the quarter. The company executed a sale of $1.9 billion in non-core student loans to enhance NIM, EPS, and ROTCE, with $200 million already sold and the remainder to settle over the year.
  • The Private Bank reported strong growth, with deposits reaching $8.7 billion and assets under management (AUM) at $5.2 billion, including the addition of new teams in Florida, Southern California, and New Jersey.
  • CFO John Woods reported EPS of $0.77 and ROTCE of 9.6%. He noted NIM improvement was driven by non-core runoff and better deposit cost management. The company executed $200 million in share buybacks during the quarter, supported by a strong CET1 ratio of 10.64%.

Outlook

  • Management reaffirmed its full-year EPS guidance and expects net interest income to grow by 3% in Q2 2025, driven by a 5 basis point improvement in NIM. Non-interest income is projected to increase mid- to high-single digits, reflecting capital markets and wealth management growth.
  • The company continues to target a year-end NIM range of 3.05% to 3.10%, supported by ongoing benefits from non-core runoff and improved deposit costs.

Financial Results

  • Net interest income declined 1.5% quarter-over-quarter due to fewer days in the period and lower interest-earning assets, despite an improved NIM.
  • Non-interest income fell 3.5% sequentially, reflecting seasonal declines in capital markets and card fees. M&A and loan syndication activity in the capital markets were impacted by market uncertainty, but pipelines for M&A are at record highs.
  • Period-end deposits grew by approximately $3 billion, with the Private Bank contributing $1.7 billion of that increase. Interest-bearing deposit costs decreased by 18 basis points sequentially.

Q&A

  • John Pancari, Evercore ISI: Questioned loan demand trends and the impact of pre-tariff inventory builds. CFO John Woods noted increased line utilization in commercial banking and strong performance in residential and home equity lending, as well as consistent growth in the Private Bank.
  • Ken Usdin, Autonomous Research: Asked about capital markets fee guidance and offsets in a challenging environment. CEO Van Saun cited a diverse fee structure and highlighted strong M&A pipelines, while CFO Woods emphasized cost controls as a potential offset to slower M&A activity.
  • Erika Najarian, UBS: Asked about the medium-term reserve outlook. CFO Woods explained that the economic forecast includes a mild recession assumption with an unemployment rate of 5.1% for most portfolios, but higher for CRE-related reserves.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed cautious optimism about loan demand and capital markets recovery but raised concerns about macroeconomic uncertainty and its impact on growth.
  • Management maintained a confident tone during prepared remarks, emphasizing resilience and strategic progress, but displayed a more cautious approach during Q&A when discussing economic risks and deal pipelines.
  • Compared to Q4 2024, management's tone reflected increased confidence in NIM trajectory and Private Bank performance, while analysts remained consistent in their scrutiny of macro challenges.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • NIM improved by 3 basis points in Q1 2025 versus 10 basis points in Q4 2024.
  • Loan growth of 1% in Q1 2025 contrasts with the subdued activity in prior quarters.
  • Analysts' focus shifted from broader strategic initiatives to specific concerns about capital markets and economic uncertainties.
  • Management showed greater confidence in achieving year-end NIM targets and Private Bank contributions compared to the previous quarter.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management identified economic uncertainty, slower capital markets recovery, and potential credit quality concerns as key risks.
  • Analysts questioned the impact of delayed M&A activity and higher unemployment assumptions on financial projections.
  • The company highlighted its strategic flexibility in managing costs and optimizing its balance sheet to mitigate potential headwinds.

Final Takeaway

Citizens Financial Group delivered a solid Q1 2025 performance with notable NIM improvement and Private Bank growth. Management reaffirmed its year-end NIM target of 3.05% to 3.10% and highlighted strategic initiatives to navigate macroeconomic challenges. Analysts remained cautiously optimistic, with attention on capital markets recovery and economic risks.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Comstock surges after entering solar recycling partnership with RWE
data.description:
Comstock (NYSE:LODE) +14.9% in Wednesday's trading after saying it signed a master services agreement to provide renewable energy company RWE (OTCPK:RWEOY) with recycling, decommissioning, and logistics services for its U.S. solar installations.

Under the deal terms, Comstock Metals (NYSE:LODE) will serve as RWE's (OTCPK:RWEOY) preferred partner for projects including the recycling of solar panels and related equipment, logistics management, eco-friendly disposal practices, and the safe transportation of materials.

Comstock (LODE) said the agreement builds on the existing partnership between the two companies after working together on multiple projects in Nevada and California, already coordinating the decommissioning, transportation, and recycling of more than 4M lbs of end-of-life solar materials for RWE (OTCPK:RWEOY), with more anticipated as demand for responsible recycling grows.

Comstock (LODE) "complements RWE's efforts as a trusted provider in the renewable energy market, ensuring environmentally conscious recycling of the solar panels and their components," Comstock Metals President Fortunato Villamagna said.

data.title: S&P, Nasdaq fall on tech rout amid more trade war concerns, Dow seesaws
data.description:
Major averages pared some of their losses on Wednesday, amid worries about a U.S.-China trade war rose on news that Nvidia will need a special license to ship graphic processing units to China.

The S&P 500 (SP500) was last -0.6%, while the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was -1.4%. The Dow (DJI) had turned marginally higher.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) was down 2 basis points to 4.32%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) was lower by 5 basis points to 3.80%.

The core retail sales beat consensus in March, while the headline number came in line

Wall Street on Tuesday ended slightly lower in a choppy trading session, as a boost from positive bank earnings petered out amid more caution over developments surrounding tariffs.

"After a strong start to the week, the market mood turned more negative again yesterday, as tensions between the US and China showed signs of further escalation," Deutsche Bank's Henry Allen said.

"So after a brief period of greater stability in markets, that reminded investors about the ongoing risks of escalation, raising fears that the trade war could still get worse from here," Allen added.

Nvidia (NVDA) fell 6.5% as it received a notice from the U.S. government that it needs a special license to ship its H20 graphic processing units or related hardware to China, and such a license will be required indefinitely.

"Some politicians may believe trade is more important than it is. Trade does matter economically, but a 'made in China' product in a store does not mean all the price paid goes to China," UBS' Paul Donovan said.

President Donald Trump has also directed an investigation to determine if U.S. critical minerals imports "threaten to impair national security," which could lead to tariffs or restrictions on the sector.

Also on the economic side, industrial production dipped slightly more than expected in March to 0.3% M/M, weaker than the -0.2% consensus figure.

Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell is also expected to speak later in the day.
